title: India's Airplane Problem: Too Few Big Planes
summary: India has a big problem with airplanes. Willie Walsh, who will soon be the boss of IndiGo, India's biggest airline, says it's a 'scandal' that India only has 50 widebody aircraft. Widebody planes are large, twin-aisle airplanes that can carry many people on long trips, like to other countries. India has 1.4 billion people and is growing fast, so it needs more of these big planes to connect people by air. Walsh talked to the BBC about this issue. He didn't comment on IndiGo's recent problems, where they had to cancel thousands of flights because of new rules for pilots. Instead, he focused on how India's aviation market is expanding but is held back by not having enough widebody aircraft. This shortage makes it hard for people to travel long distances without stopping. Walsh believes India should have many more widebody planes to match its size and potential.

background_read: India is a big country with over 1.4 billion people, and more and more people want to fly on airplanes to travel far away. Widebody aircraft are special big planes that can fly long distances without stopping, like from India to America or Europe. Right now, India only has 50 of these big planes, which is not enough for all the people who want to fly. Willie Walsh, who knows a lot about airlines, says this is a big problem because India should have many more to help people connect by air. This article talks about why having more widebody aircraft is important for India's future.
Article_Structure: WHO: Willie Walsh, the incoming chief executive of IndiGo, and India's aviation sector
WHAT: India has only 50 widebody aircraft, which is called a 'scandal' because it limits long-haul travel
WHY: To highlight the need for more widebody aircraft to support India's growing population and aviation market

perspectives.description: He thinks India's lack of widebody aircraft is a scandal and that the country needs more to improve connectivity.
